original_abstract: The actualization of women rights has been a concern worldwide.Decades of outcry of dehumanization, degradation, humiliation, exclusion, discriminations, arbitrary deprivation of liberty and gender-based violence have led to numerous efforts at curbing these issues at the international, regional, national level and through legal framework, conferences, conventions, programmes.Similarly, there has been a rise in social advocacy groups at national, regional and global level.Yet, the problem is far from being ameliorated much less eliminated.This paper presents a descriptive analysis, based on literature review, of the major issues in women's right.It examines the various debacles to actualizing women's right one of which is patriarchy, and the inability of State to implement many of the legal frameworks.The paper finds that a major cause of women's right violation is the financial dependency of women on their male counterparts.It recommends, as a departure from previous writing which demands more responsibility from State, the need for women's responsibility.This is because social advocacy for women's right without social responsibility from women will produce affirmative action for women who have opportunities they never explore.This does not seek to undermine the policy recommendations in other literature rather it is a call to rise above and beyond limitations through self-motivating steps that places a demand on women's abilities and capacities.
humanized_abstract: Women rights are changing rapidly all over the world, and decades of campaigning to end dehumanization, degradation, humiliation, exclusion, discrimination, arbitrary deprivation of liberty and gender-based violence and violence against women and girls, among other things, have led to a plethora of local, regional and international activities to address these problems through various legal instruments, conferences and programmes, as well as through civil society organisations, locally, regionally and internationally. Despite these numerous efforts women rights violation continues to persist. The aim of this paper is to carry out a descriptive exploratory analysis of women rights violation problems, drawn from literature review, that discusses the various challenges that emanate in the struggle for women rights, in particular the role of patriarchy and State failure to fully implement national legislation for women rights protection. Findings of the study indicate that women rights violation is the result of women being economically dependent on men.The study therefore recommends that women themselves must take responsibility for ending their economic dependence on men, a call different from the previous studies which recommend that it is the role of the State to enforce laws that protect women rights. The reason behind this call is that women empowerment through affirmative action, such as equal opportunities promoted through women rights organisations and societies, which campaign for increased opportunities for women who may have the potential, yet who do not utilise the opportunities provided, will continue to remain ineffective if women themselves do not embrace responsibility for their situation.
